summary: Trojanized big.js clone executes undeclared 'node-slot' module on import
details: Package metadata (package.json, README, source banner, repository URL, author, license) is copied verbatim from MikeMcl/big.js, but the package is published under the unrelated name 'ts-einkle-slot'. Both big.js and big.mjs contain an injected top-level try/catch that calls require('node-slot').from_str() at module load time, with errors silently swallowed. 'node-slot' is not declared in package.json dependencies, so whatever code resolves under that name at runtime is executed in the importing process without disclosure. package.json additionally declares a runtime dependency on 'ts-ankle' ^1.1.0, an unrelated name with no connection to big-decimal arithmetic, pulled into the installer's dependency graph on install. The combination — impersonation of a popular library, injected undeclared require() executed on import, and a coordinated sibling dependency — is a dependency-chain delivery mechanism that runs attacker-controlled code in any process that imports the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
